profile.name: Mahatav Arora
profile.intro: I'm a freelance developer you can count on to build standout websites and dependable AI systems—and to ship them. I take projects from first brief to production launch, and I sweat the details so the result works for the people who use it.
profile.availability: Available for freelance web and AI builds—dependable from first brief to launch

featured.title: Channeling Skinny Bob
featured.summary: A cinematic content platform that turns a creator’s niche archive into a world their audience can explore.
featured.description: I took this project from a loose creative brief to a production launch. That meant shaping the visual direction, building the responsive Astro experience, and organizing a large research library. I also engineered a secret command-line archive and handled the private evidence-upload API and deployment. It is the kind of freelance work I love: distinctive on the surface, carefully engineered underneath, and built around what the client’s audience should feel and do.

featured.title: TA Application System
featured.summary: A Django hiring workflow with AI resume extraction and coordinator-grade filtering.
featured.description: Connected an Ollama-backed FastAPI extraction service to a Django application. From there I surfaced validated skills in the application flow, expanded coordinator filters, and configured the multi-service development stack.

featured.title: Green Construction
featured.summary: A multi-page publishing system for sustainable construction research, events, people, and funding.
featured.description: Redesigned and maintained the Green Construction Research & Training Center site. I added custom page indexing and search, repaired the contact workflow, and maintained the Wilden Living Lab site.

featured.title: GPT-2, From Scratch
featured.summary: A tested GPT-2-style language model implementation, from tokenizer to generation loop.
featured.description: Implemented embeddings, causal multi-head attention, transformer blocks, MLPs, and weight tying. The training and inference side covers AdamW, BPE and character tokenization, checkpointing, evaluation, and top-k/top-p generation.

featured.title: OKGN Boat Rentals
featured.summary: A faster, clearer rental website and multi-step booking flow built for peak-season traffic.
featured.description: Rebuilt the customer journey across fleet, reviews, FAQ, crew, and booking pages. The booking system handles date and time selection, vessels, and passenger details. It also covers validation, spam protection, rate limiting, and PHP email delivery.

experiments.title: Autonomous Drone Person Tracking
experiments.summary: Real-time YOLOv8 person detection mapped to a 3×3 autonomous flight-control grid.

experiments.title: Computer Vision Spider Bot
experiments.summary: YOLOv7 and BYTETracker target following connected to ESP32 movement control.

experiments.title: Autonomous Landing Pad Detection
experiments.summary: A UAV landing prototype that combines HSV masks, moments, Hough circles, and OCR. DroneKit logic ties the detections to flight control.

experiments.title: Banking eKYC Prototypes
experiments.summary: Identity-research workflows for face capture, labelling, and detection. They also cover facial landmarks and head-pose estimation.

research.title: Corpus-Conditioned LLM Pre-Training
research.summary: Trained parallel GPT-2-style models on Eastern and Western philosophical corpora. I then measured how their outputs diverged across 284 prompts and 14 categories.
research.paperTitle: Corpus-Conditioned Bias: Training GPT-2 Models on Eastern and Western Philosophical Texts

research.title: Human vs. AI Code Analytics
research.summary: Fine-tuned CodeBERT classifiers and built repository scanners for capstone projects. Each scanner estimates whether a source file is human-written, AI-generated, or uncertain.
research.paperTitle: Pre-Post AI Comparisons: Behavioural Changes in Student Capstone Projects

research.title: Software Process Analysis
research.summary: Built a GitHub analytics pipeline covering pull requests, commits, reviews, and branches. It also surfaces communication patterns and process-model visualizations.

research.title: Jersey Number Recognition
research.summary: Built and evaluated a multi-stage SoccerNet pipeline for reading jersey numbers. The stages combine NAFNet restoration, YOLOv8 pose crops, PARSeq, vision-language models, and confidence-weighted tracklet aggregation.
research.paperTitle: Jersey Number Recognition in Broadcast Soccer Video

faq.question: What kind of projects do you take on?
faq.answer: Standout websites, full-stack systems, and practical AI/ML tools—from creative client builds to production research pipelines. If it lives on the web or runs a model, I can build and ship it.

faq.question: Where are you based, and do you work remotely?
faq.answer: I'm based in Kelowna, BC, and work remotely with clients and teams anywhere.

faq.question: Are you available for new work?
faq.answer: Yes—I'm available for freelance web and AI projects, and I'm reliable about timelines and communication. The contact form below is the fastest way to start.

faq.question: What technologies do you work with?
faq.answer: Day to day I work with Astro, Django, FastAPI, PyTorch, and Docker, across Python and JavaScript.
